feed.title: kolm changelog
feed.subtitle: Release notes, spec changes, benchmarks, and production fixes for kolm.

entry.title: v6.5 - Self-serve everything, depth where it was thin
entry.summary: External 7.5/10 review called out shallow depth on three axes: API surface, failure mode docs, and the compile pipeline. v6.5 closes all three. /api is a full Bearer-auth reference for every /v1 endpoint with request and response schemas, error codes, and SDK install lines for npm, pip, brew. /failure-modes documents twelve named paths (FM-001 through FM-303) with exit codes, HTTP status, artifact and receipt state, and operator action for every one. /how-it-works is the eight-stage compile deep-dive: gather, spec, synthesize, k-sample, verify, fit, observe, sign - each with the inputs, outputs, and verifier-type taxonomy. Enterprise was decoupled from email: $2,999/mo, fully self-serve from /signup?plan=enterprise. BAA, DPA, and SOC 2 Type I letter live at /trust and download on signup. Healthcare, finance, faq, cloud, troubleshooting, integrations all rewritten to remove mailto-led CTAs in favor of self-serve flows. Homepage gained a three-box explainer (inputs - compile - runtime) above the cred-band so first-time readers understand the artifact before the credibility band lands. Dashboard ships a plan-management card backed by /v1/account/change-plan, /v1/account/cancel, /v1/account/delete, with Stripe billing-portal hand-off. Annual billing toggle on /pricing confirmed intact from v5.4.

entry.title: v6.4 - Hero reframed, outcome over file-count
entry.summary: Same-day feedback after v6.3: the "in a single file" framing leads with the implementation detail, not the outcome. Buyers care that they get a working AI that's theirs, not whether it ships as one artifact or many. v6.4 reframes the hero to "Your own AI, compiled to your task." The lede shifts from "compiles a task into a file you own" to "compiles a task into a working AI you own." Title, description, OG card, Twitter meta, /launch page and /og-card.svg all updated. The portability proof (one runnable artifact, ships through your pipelines, runs on a laptop / phone / VPC) survives in the supporting copy where it earns its keep.

entry.title: v6.3 - Install path lit, bench numbers pinned
entry.summary: The launch blocker from v6.2 is closed. github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack is now public, MIT-licensed, with the runtime SDK, the RS-1 spec, the GitHub Action, 26 example sets, an offline test suite, and reproducible bench numbers from a known-good fixture. npm i -g github:sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack resolves end-to-end on a fresh machine and produces a working kolm binary in under ten seconds. The repo is the curated public install surface, not the company codebase: strategy docs, internal benchmarks, the cloud server, and Stripe/Vercel/Railway plumbing all stayed private. Reproducible bench numbers at docs/benchmark-results-v0.1.0.md: n=100, p50=311us, p95=431us, egress_attempts=0. Six offline tests at test/smoke.test.js covering tamper detection (fatal) and soft-fail signature (non-fatal). CI matrix on Linux + macOS + Windows for Node 20 and 22.

entry.title: v6.2 - Villain named, why-now surfaced
entry.summary: Three independent launch reviews landed within 24 hours. They confirmed v6.1's empathy-led hero and called out the next gap: the homepage names the product but not the villain. v6.2 sharpens the pain section to that frame: every requests.post("api.openai.com") in your code is a liability. The four-row table is now cost / privacy / reproducibility / continuity, each named with the explicit regulatory frame (HIPAA, SR 11-7, EU AI Act, GDPR). Added a "Why this is happening now" primary CTA pulling readers into /why-now where the three-forces argument lives. Survival pass on /quickstart (dropped v0 hedge), /docs (dropped v0 hedge in meta description), /compile (dropped Sprint 1 internal language), /trust (rephrased CSP-nonce roadmap line). GitHub repo 404 flagged as launch blocker - resolved same day in v6.3.

entry.title: v6.1 - Hero restored, empathy-led
entry.summary: Brought back "Your own AI, in a single file." as the homepage hero. The v6.0 hero ("AI you can sign, audit, and run anywhere") tested too technical for non-regulated buyers - "sign" and "audit" are jargon that punish a first-time reader. The new lede answers the only question that matters: why a single file? Because it bundles the model, your examples, the evaluator, and a signed receipt for every output, packaged like a Docker image you can ship through your existing pipelines, version in git, and run on a laptop, a phone, or inside your VPC. Sign-and-audit framing demoted to the credibility band lower on the page where regulated readers expect it. Title, description, OG, and Twitter meta tags all updated to match.

entry.title: v6.0 - Regulated-buyer launch polish
entry.summary: Hero rewritten for regulated buyers: "AI you can sign, audit, and run anywhere." Header and footer pinned to a single 1180px wrap so navigation stops drifting between tabs. New regulated-industries strip on the homepage with explicit doors into healthcare, finance, and government and defense. Defensive language stripped from 12 pages: "roadmap" hedges and "honest" framings replaced with the actual product behavior. Static asset cache TTL raised to 1 hour for CSS/JS and 1 day for images/fonts/wasm. SOC 2 Type I Q4 2026 unified across pricing, trust, healthcare, finance, faq. Final fake-claim sweep: unpublished @kolmogorov/recipe-mcp, kolm-ai/tap homebrew, winget kolm, ghcr.io/sneaky-hippo/kolm Docker image, "pypi soon" speculation all retired in favor of the real GitHub-source install path. Manifesto and FAQ deduped of paired hedged+confident paragraphs.

entry.title: v5.9 - Benchmark scope pass
entry.summary: Pulled every "+15.33pp on SWE-bench Lite" reference off the public surface. /benchmarks now documents the kolm-benchmark-1 harness end-to-end. Homepage credibility band swapped to the four guarantees the artifact actually carries: Q >= 0.85 compile-time gate, egress=0 enforced by the harness, microsecond local-exec latency, HMAC-chained receipt integrity. The +15.33pp run was real - it was REM hooks runtime context-injection on SWE-bench Lite n=150, not a kolm artifact. v5.9 separated the two.

entry.title: v5.8 - GTM motion live
entry.summary: Three-phase motion is a public surface. /motion shows developer bottoms-up, vertical enterprise land-and-expand, edge / embedded OEM. /roi is a real calculator with three presets and teacher-cost-honest math. Home leads with the compiler-positioning line.

entry.title: v5.7 - Launch readiness pass
entry.summary: Comparators (vs Ollama, RAG, fine-tune). Why-now and threat-model pages. Trust, integrations, glossary, press. Articles RSS. brew + winget stubs. Cursor rules and Claude Code skill. Anti-incumbent hero. Live registry counter. Header + wrap unified across every page.

entry.title: v5.5 - Mobile pass hotfix
entry.summary: Kill horizontal scroll on iPhone. Fix brand-dot duplication on canonical pages. Hide nav under 920px so the header stops overflowing.

entry.title: v5.4 - Launch ready
entry.summary: Mobile audit, Teams $149 tier, /architecture /launch /troubleshooting /cookbook /legal /edge pages. Python SDK, GitHub Action, Vercel + Railway live.

entry.title: v5.3 - Brand system, benchmark page, and production test isolation
entry.summary: Refined the private AI compiler positioning, refreshed the logo and visual system, added the benchmark protocol page, isolated e2e tests from real data, and hardened hosted admin-key behavior.

entry.title: v5.2 - Positioning, ICP pages, manifesto, FAQ, quickstart
entry.summary: Reframed the homepage around private local AI artifacts, added ICP landing pages, manifesto, FAQ, quickstart, and a public changelog.

entry.title: v5.0 - Pricing, security trust chain, manual, privacy, terms
entry.summary: Shipped the v5 site sweep with pricing, security, docs, manual, privacy, terms, and custom error pages.

entry.title: RS-1 v0.1 schemas published
entry.summary: Published the manifest and receipt schemas for signed .kolm artifacts.
